visible_agent_inputs.question: In what year did the star of "Flesh and the Devil" receive an Academy Honorary Award?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1: Passages define the Academy Honorary Award and give example recipients: e.g., passage 1 says the Honorary Award "is given annually by the Board of Governors of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences" and that many recipients are "Classic Hollywood stars, such as Lillian Gish, Barbara Stanwyck, Kirk Douglas, and Lauren Bacall." Other passages note individual recipients (e.g., "he did receive an Academy Honorary Award" for Sidney Lumet). None of the passages identify who starred in "Flesh and the Devil" or connect that film's star to an Honorary Award. Next hop: retrieve the star of "Flesh and the Devil" (bridge entity) and then retrieve whether and in what year that person received an Academy Honorary Award — use sources that list the film's cast and the Academy Honorary Award recipients/years.

current_output.query: Flesh and the Devil star Academy Honorary Award year

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Academy Honorary Award | The Academy Honorary Award – instituted in 1948 for the 21st Academy Awards (previously called the Special Award, which was first presented in early 1929) – is given annually by the Board of Governors of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences (AMPAS) to celebrate motion picture achievements that are not covered by existing Academy Awards, although prior winners of competitive Academy Awards are not excluded from receiving the Honorary Award. Unless otherwise spe ...[truncated]
Devil in the Flesh 2 | Devil in the Flesh 2 is the 2000 sequel to "Devil in the Flesh" which starred Rose McGowan.

Devil in the Flesh (1998 film) | Devil in the Flesh is a 1998 American thriller film starring Rose McGowan. The film was also released under the title "Dearly Devoted". It was co-scripted by Kelly Carlin-McCall but is not based on the twice-filmed Raymond Radiguet novel "Le Diable au corps" ("The Devil in the Flesh").

Devil in the Flesh (1947 film) | Devil in the Flesh (French: Le diable au corps ) is a 1947 French movie directed by Claude Autant-Lara starring Micheline Presle and Gérard Philipe. It is based on a novel by Raymond Radiguet.

Flesh and the Devil | Flesh and the Devil (1926) is a romantic drama silent film released by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer and stars Greta Garbo, John Gilbert, Lars Hanson, and Barbara Kent, directed by Clarence Brown, and based on the novel "The Undying Past" by Hermann Sudermann.

The World, the Flesh, the Devil | The World, the Flesh, the Devil is a 1932 British crime film directed by George A. Cooper and starring Harold Huth, Isla Bevan and Victor Stanley. It was based on a play by Laurence Cowen. A lawyer plans to murder an aristocrat and steal his inheritance.

Robert Honorary Award | The Robert Honorary Award (Danish: "Æres-Robert" , Honorary Robert) has been given since 1986 as one of the Robert Awards (Danish: "Robert prisen" ) by the Danish Film Academy. It is the Danish equivalent of the American Academy Honorary Award.
source_state.summary_2: Flesh and the Devil (1926) stars Greta Garbo, John Gilbert, Lars Hanson, and Barbara Kent (passage 5). The supplied passages do not state whether any of these performers received an Academy Honorary Award (further retrieval is needed to find a year, if any).

Garbo was nominated three times for the Academy Award for Best Actress and received an Academy Honorary Award in 1954 for her "luminous and unforgettable screen performances." In 1999, the American Film Institute ranked Garbo fifth on their list of the greatest female stars of classic Hollywood cinema, after Katharine Hepburn, Bette ...[truncated]

Flesh and the Devil | Flesh and the Devil (1926) is a romantic drama silent film released by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer and stars Greta Garbo, John Gilbert, Lars Hanson, and Barbara Kent, directed by Clarence Brown, and based on the novel "The Undying Past" by Hermann Sudermann.

Love (1927 American film) | Love (1927) is a silent film directed by Edmund Goulding and released by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer. MGM made the film in order to capitalize on its winning romantic team of Greta Garbo and John Gilbert who had starred in the 1926 blockbuster, "Flesh and the Devil".

Academy Honorary Award | The Academy Honorary Award – instituted in 1948 for the 21st Academy Awards (previously called the Special Award, which was first presented in early 1929) – is given annually by the Board of Governors of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences (AMPAS) to celebrate motion picture achievements that are not covered by existing Academy Awards, although prior winners of competitive Academy Awards are not excluded from receiving the Honorary Award. Unless otherwise spe ...[truncated]

The Kiss (1929 film) | The Kiss is a 1929 American silent drama film from Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer directed by Jacques Feyder and starring Greta Garbo, Conrad Nagel and Lew Ayres in his first feature film. The film is known for being both MGM's and Greta Garbo's last silent film. It was also the last such film for Conrad Nagel. That said, the film did take advantage of the new sound technology, and was released with an orchestral score and sound effects. It was based on an 1896 film.

Ernest Lehman | Ernest Paul Lehman (December 8, 1915 – July 2, 2005) was an American screenwriter. He was nominated six times for Academy Awards for his screenplays during his career, but did not win. At the 73rd Academy Awards in 2001, he received an Honorary Academy Award in recognition of his achievements and his influential works for the screen. His work inspired new generations of screenwriters and captivated filmmakers, actors, film critics, and audiences. He was the first screenwriter to  ...[truncated]

Garbo (film) | Garbo is a 1992 Australian comedy film directed by Ron Cobb. It was written by and starring the Australian comedians Neill Gladwin and Steve Kearney; Max Cullen also stars. Filmed in Melbourne, the story focuses on two incompetent Australian garbagemen ("garbos" in Australian slang) who admire Greta Garbo and have to compete with a super-efficient new outfit.
